title: The Tenth Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## August 14, 2022 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

---

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The Tenth Sunday after Pentecost falls within Ordinary Time (the long season between Pentecost and Advent). Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings would likely include 1 Kings 2:10-12, 3:3-14 (Solomon's prayer for wisdom); Psalm 111; Ephesians 5:15-20 (living as children of light); and John 6:51-58 (Jesus as the bread of life, continuing the Bread of Life discourse). This Sunday emphasizes wisdom, discernment, and spiritual nourishment during the formative post-Pentecost period of the church year.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the immediate aftermath of General Convention 2022, held in Baltimore just weeks prior (July 7-10), where significant votes on LGBTQ+ inclusion and reparations for slavery had generated both celebration and controversy within the denomination. The Diocese of Colorado, under Bishop Kym Lucas (elected in 2018), was navigating these national church decisions at the diocesan level. Saint John's Cathedral itself, as the seat of the diocesan bishop, would have been particularly attuned to implementing and interpreting these recent national church actions.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing typical mid-August heat with drought conditions persisting across Colorado following an exceptionally dry spring and early summer. The city was focused on school year preparations as students prepared to return in the coming weeks. Locally, Denver's homeless encampment policies and downtown revitalization remained ongoing civic concerns, with the Capitol Hill area adjacent to the cathedral experiencing its typical gentrification tensions.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

President Biden signed the Inflation Reduction Act on August 16, 2022 (two days after this sermon), a landmark $369 billion climate and healthcare bill; this vote had dominated the previous week's news cycle as it passed the Senate on August 7. The ongoing investigation into former President Trump intensified with the FBI's Mar-a-Lago search on August 8, dominating cable news and political discourse. Monkeypox was declared a Public Health Emergency of International Concern by the WHO on July 23, and U.S. cases continued rising, creating ambient anxiety about disease.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Russia's invasion of Ukraine continued with intense fighting in the Donbas region and Ukrainian counteroffensive efforts; the war dominated international headlines as a major humanitarian and geopolitical crisis. The UK was in the midst of its Conservative Party leadership election following Boris Johnson's resignation, with Liz Truss and Rishi Sunak as the final two candidates (results came August 5). Flooding in Pakistan from monsoon rains was becoming a catastrophic humanitarian disaster in early-to-mid August, eventually affecting one-third of the country.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

The nation was experiencing acute partisan polarization and institutional anxiety, with the Trump FBI search dominating social media and cable news in a way that felt destabilizing to many. There was underlying economic unease despite job growth, as inflation remained high and Americans worried about recession. The summer doldrums mixed with these anxieties created a contemplative, somewhat weary national mood as people returned from vacations; for a cathedral congregation, Greer's sermon on wisdom and spiritual nourishment would have landed in a moment when people were seeking grounding and deeper meaning amid turbulent circumstances.
